item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Romanian tree.jpg
Author: Innano1
Release year: 2011

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first thought is there's another article in the wide, wide world of the wiki that does basically the same thing, appearing to people to give them stuff that's potentially dangerous. My second thought is I love that the DATA EXPUNGED is actually part of the note; that's one of the funniest footnotes I've ever seen. My third thought is the "D-Class uprising" is kind of silly, if only because it's more or less described as such. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FWIW, even though I downvoted this I'm fine with you crosslinking my work to it. SCP-2890 might be especially appropriate given its propensity to escape via unlikely coincidence and its association with a holiday pop song.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can feel free to link to any of mine for crosslinks anytime. If you like I can edit something into the page you link to as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point. Added a sentence saying how the Foundation has no knowledge of 2536 appearing to anyone else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation just assumes this is only appearing to Foundation personnel? That seems incompetent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally feel Euclid would suit it better (maybe a high-level Euclid), but if you think it should be Keter, then by all means keep it that way :)

The classification doesn't detract from the overall piece, it's really good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Call me Scrooge, but I'm not really feeling this. The "wish fulfilment causes chaos" story isn't given enough of a fresh twist to engage me, and yet another GoI that knows all about the Foundation and can't be stopped by its security is a bit repetitive. Also, I see you there, explanatory note at the end - just because you're trying to hide in the middle doesn't fool me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'I Wish It Could Be Christmas Everyday'

*Black Mirror flashbacks intensify*

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My justification for making it Keter was that, even if it itself doesn't threaten human life, it can cause breaches of SCP's that are a threat to human life. If people agree, though, I'd be happy to change it to Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd be happy to crosslink, but I didn't want to interfere with the stories of other SCP's by having them escape via Christmas tree. If you have any suggestions regarding feasible SCP's, I'd be happy to implement them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it but I feel like the blackboxed SCP numbers are a missed opportunity for crosslinkage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NOT THE AUTHOR

I asked Tanhony that as well when i was reading through and the explanation was that what if it appears before Able or some other keter scp and helps them achieve their darkest goal.

An merry upvote from me as well. Shame about SCP-XMAS though :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Turns around and glares suspiciously at my Christmas tree*

Really well done, and in the holiday spirit!

Though one little nitpick, I'm not sure about the Keter classification. I don't think it actively poses a threat to human life. Sure, you can't contain it, and it has helped D-Class revolt and SCPs escape, but it's only a problem during December. I'd make it Euclid, but in the end, it's your choice, and if you want to supply evidence why you think it should be Keter, then by all means do :)

Still, it gets an upboat from me :)
